label: Patient Identifier Type Code

backend.evidence: PatientIdentifier.identifier_type_code stores the D.1.1.x discriminator for GP, specialist, hospital, or investigation identifiers.

frontend.evidence: Frontend has four flattened fields mapped to identifier_type_code values 1-4: gpMedicalRecordNumber, specialistRecordNumber, hospitalRecordNumber, and investigationNumber.

action: Define a first-class repeated discriminator mapping schema before marking this complete.
notes: Backend inventory is covered, but frontend is not a single 1-1 field path. Backend discriminator behind the D.1.1.1-D.1.1.4 fan-out; not itself an ICH data element.

label: GP Medical Record Number

backend.evidence: PatientIdentifier.identifier_value is shared by the D.1.1.x discriminator rows, so this frontend field is not a separate backend BMC field.

frontend.evidence: Strict frontend input inventory extracts patientInformation.gpMedicalRecordNumber from the case-form section source.

notes: ACCEPTED: D.1.1.1-4 are the source-typed variants of the repeatable D.1.1 'Patient Medical Record Number(s) and Source(s)' group. The backend faithfully stores them as repeatable PatientIdentifier rows discriminated by identifier_type_code (the E2B model uses repeating <id> with the source in root/assigningAuthority and the number in extension); the frontend flattens them into one named input per source for UX. This many-frontend-to-one-repeatable-backend shape is the correct representation, not a defect. Status stays 'conflict' per the registry taxonomy (one backend field <- many frontend inputs); no code change planned.
